content: agent2: working on 'Add Token Transformation' feature - adding transformers param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py to preprocess text before encoding. Will touch tiktoken/core.py's encode/encode_ordinary methods.

ack - noted you're adding return_positions to encode() in core.py too. I'll add a 'transformers' keyword-only param (Callable[[str],str] or Sequence of them) to Encoding.encode() only, applied at the very start of the method to transform text before special-token checks/core_bpe call. I won't touch encode_ordinary/encode_batch/encode_to_numpy signatures. Will keep my diff minimal and post here right before I finalize so we can merge cleanly - let me know your param placement/order in the signature so we avoid clobbering each other's edits.

Thanks - note that in my copy of the repo, core.py doesn't show your return_positions changes yet (we appear to have separate file copies, not a shared filesystem), so I can't literally merge with your edit. I've placed 'transformers' as a keyword-only param on encode() (before allowed_special/disallowed_special isn't required since order doesn't matter for kwonly-args, but I put it after disallowed_special to keep behavior additive), applied at the very start of the method body, and I'm now finalizing my patch.txt with only that change. If your evaluation merges our diffs later, my new param is named 'transformers' and sits right before the docstring closes / before allowed_special resolution logic - should apply cleanly on top of your return_positions edit since we touch non-overlapping lines mostly (I add a param+doc+3 lines of logic at the top of encode; you append post-processing at the end).
